figures.caption: Figure 2: Stage 1 vs. Stage 2 across temporal slices. Hit@10 (%) over slices 𝐷0–𝐷5 for NQ and MS MARCO under Expanded and Fixed protocols. Dashed lines denote Stage 1 adaptation before PAMT; solid lines denote Stage 2 after PAMT. The 𝐷0 point corresponds to the initially trained model. Colors indicate method/docid combinations.

Figure 3: Stage 1 vs. Stage 2 aggregate continual-learning metrics. AP, FWTdiag, and signed BWT ± are computed from MRR@10 and reported in percentage points for NQ and MS MARCO under Expanded and Fixed protocols. Hatched bars denote Stage 1 adaptation before PAMT; dotted bars denote Stage 2 after PAMT.

Figure 1: Post-adaptation memory tuning (PAMT) for continual GenIR. (a) Adapt-then-stabilize pipeline: The parametric memory head (PMH) is attached and co-trained with the backbone onD0 to establish the initial addressing mechanism. At each session 𝑡 ≥1, the backbone is adapted on the new sliceD𝑡 (Stage 1), then frozen and refined via a memory-only stabilization stage (Stage 2). Stage 2 updates a sparse subset of PMH value rows to improve retention on legacy slices D0:𝑡−1 without gradient-based training on legacy relevance supervision. (b) Parametric memory head: During prefix-trie constrained docid decoding, decoder hidden states are projected by 𝑓𝜙 to perform product-key top-𝐾mem retrieval from the key tables (𝐾 (1) , 𝐾 (2) ) and latent value table 𝑉 hid. Retrieved values are aggregated into a latent correction 𝑏hid 𝑘 in decoder hidden space; its effect on token scores is computed via the frozen output embedding matrix over trie-valid tokens 𝐴𝑘 (𝜋) only. (c) Sparse PMH updates: Decoding-time access logs from the current session X𝑡 are contrasted with a historical access sketch to score rows using access frequency and inverse historical frequency (AF×IHF), forming an update set Ω𝑡 . Sparsity is enforced by updating only 𝑉 hid [𝑛] for 𝑛 ∈Ω𝑡 while blocking gradients on protected rows P𝑡 ; the adapted backbone, 𝑓𝜙 , and keys remain fixed during Stage 2, keeping the Stage 2 routing function stable.

Table 2: RQ4 (Expanded): Comparison to prior approaches. Diagonal entries report per-slice Hit@10 (%) after each session under the Expanded protocol, using constrained decoding over the cumulative corpus D0:𝑡 . AP, FWTdiag, and signed BWT ± are computed from the lower-triangular MRR@10 matrix (Section 5.3). Best values in each column are underlined.
